optimizationReadiness.reason: Experiment did not move the target. The same 6-trace cluster (q2, q8, q9, q10, q12, q17) still exits with zero grounded tool calls. The auto-bind escape hatch was not triggered for these traces, indicating these queries do not reach the `preserveNamedTargetClarification` partial-coverage branch in query-clarification.ts. The root cause for this cluster lies at an earlier decision point (likely the upstream clarification-trigger gate before viable-candidate analysis, or the model's own early-return-without-tools behavior before the deterministic gate fires).
Same dominant cluster, same traceCount. No movement.
Unchanged. Same downstream symptom of toolSelection failures plus calibration artifact.
Small positive drift within reviewer noise; not attributable to the experiment.
Small positive drift within reviewer noise; not attributable to the experiment.
Above floor. Slight improvement; not attributable to the experiment (reviewer/analyzer noise).

dominantFailureClusters.label: named_target_without_resolution
dominantFailureClusters.description: Identical cluster before and after. The auto-bind escape hatch did not fire because these traces do not reach the partial-clause-coverage branch in query-clarification.ts — the clarification signal originates earlier in the pipeline.

interactionRisks: Fix site was wrong: the cluster's control flow does not pass through `preserveNamedTargetClarification` partial-coverage branch. Next hypothesis should target the earlier decision site that emits clarification_required with zero grounded tool calls (likely pre-plan probe + planner short-circuit path).
Retries in the test harness show 0 tool calls twice then clarification_required — the model itself is exiting without calling tools before any clarification gate runs.
calibrationNotes: Same analyzer calibration artifact as baseline (uniform execution=2 on successful iterative traces).
